description:
src/lib.rs:319-320 'std::str::from_utf8(value.as_bytes()).map(str::to_owned)' validates UTF-8 then allocates a fresh String. value.as_bytes() borrows from a Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> so we cannot consume the original Vec.

Idiomatic equivalent: 'String::from_utf8(value.as_bytes().to_vec())' — same number of bytes copied but a single from_utf8 + alloc instead of two function calls. Or, since we need an owned String anyway, just '.to_vec()' the bytes first and then from_utf8.

Marginal; current form is readable. Filed for completeness.
title: put() UTF-8 check uses str::from_utf8 + str::to_owned; could be String::from_utf8
